title: ALTER TABLE: Add and Remove Columns

description.task: Use ALTER TABLE to add new columns, set their defaults, and drop unused columns.
instructions: Run each block sequentially: CREATE, INSERT, first SELECT.
Run the two ALTER ADD COLUMN statements.
Run the UPDATE statements and second SELECT.
Run ALTER DROP COLUMN and the final SELECT.
Note how existing rows get the DEFAULT value for new columns.
description.hint: When you ADD a column with a DEFAULT, all existing rows immediately get that default value. Dropping a column is permanent — all data in that column is lost.
description.expectedOutput:
After ADD: 5 columns (product_id, name, price, category, stock, discount_percent)
After UPDATE: stock values filled
After DROP: category column removed

title: ALTER TABLE: Modify Column Types and Rename

description.task: Use ALTER TABLE to change a column's data type, rename a column, and rename the entire table.
instructions: Run ALTER TYPE change — verify price is now DECIMAL.
Run the second SELECT to see renamed columns.
Run RENAME TABLE and query products_v2.
In a comment: what risk exists when renaming a table in a production environment?
description.hint: In production, renaming a table breaks any application code, stored procedures, or views that reference the old table name. Always update all references before or after renaming.
description.expectedOutput:
After renames: columns are product_name, price(DECIMAL), quantity
Final query: Monitor(300), Laptop(1000), Mouse(20)

title: ALTER TABLE: Add and Drop Constraints

description.task: Use ALTER TABLE to add NOT NULL, CHECK, UNIQUE constraints to an existing table and then drop one of them.
instructions: Run CREATE, INSERT, then both ALTER ADD CONSTRAINT statements.
Uncomment one of the failing inserts and note the error.
Run DROP CONSTRAINT and the information_schema query.
description.hint: Named constraints (ADD CONSTRAINT name ...) can be dropped by name later. Anonymous constraints are harder to drop. Always name your constraints in production schemas.
description.expectedOutput:
4 employees after valid INSERT
After DROP CONSTRAINT: only PRIMARY KEY and UNIQUE remain in information_schema
